purpose: Page navigation for a partitioned collection: a nav of page items with previous/next controls, the current page marked aria-current="page", and a windowed page display with ellipses.
useWhen: A collection is split into pages and the user moves between them (search results, tables, archives).
Each page has a real URL — set href-template and every item becomes a link the browser (and crawlers, and middle-click) can follow.
Page state lives in the app — omit href-template and items are buttons surfacing the cancelable zbk-page-change event.

notWhen.case: The collection loads incrementally on one page (infinite scroll, "load more").
Sequential steps in a flow (wizard, checkout).
notWhen.instead: A step indicator — steps are a process with an order, not a partitioned collection.

guidance: One spelling per navigation intent: href-template present means links (the browser navigates, no event); absent means buttons (zbk-page-change fires and, uncanceled, the element adopts the page and announces it).
Canceling zbk-page-change (preventDefault) keeps current unchanged — the consumer owns the state and sets current itself.
The window keeps a constant width of boundaries*2 + siblings*2 + 3 items so the control never reflows while paging; an ellipsis never hides a single page.
The nav defaults its accessible name to "Pagination"; set aria-label on the element when a page has more than one pagination region.

icon.description: Replaces a drawn chevron glyph: data-position="start" is the previous control's glyph, data-position="end" the next control's — any markup: svg, icon-font glyph, HTML character, image.
guidance: The glyphs are aria-hidden; the controls keep their "Previous page" / "Next page" accessible names regardless of what renders inside them.

keyboard.does: Moves through the page and previous/next controls (native behavior, preserved). Disabled previous/next controls in button mode stay focusable via aria-disabled so the user's place is kept at the ends.
Activates the focused control (native button behavior; links activate on Enter only, as links do).

examples.title: Real URLs per page
examples.why: href-template turns every item into a real link — crawlers, middle-click, and open-in-new-tab all work; there is no event to wire.

examples.title: Compact
examples.why: Previous/next around a "Page 3 of 12" readout — for narrow containers where a page window cannot fit.

examples.title: Pagination as a stepper
examples.why: Steps are a process, not a partitioned collection: they have names, completion state, and usually cannot be jumped to freely. Use a step indicator pattern.
